generated_comment: Yaar, tu dekh na, kya zaroorat thi uss ladki ko? Pata tha Sameer married hai, bachche hain uske. Phir bhi aise chipakti rahi. Aisi auraton ki wajah se hi ghar barbaad ho jaate hain. Bechara Sameer toh bas 'fas gaya', uski koi galti nahi thi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment blames the 'other woman' entirely for an affair, stating 'she knew he was married' and describing her as 'chipakti rahi' (clinging), implying seduction. It uses family protection rhetoric ('ghar barbaad ho jaate hain') and explicitly frames the man as a helpless victim ('fas gaya', 'uski koi galti nahi thi'), demonstrating blame asymmetry.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 41. Divorced two years ago. A Punjabi man working as a real estate developer in Chandigarh. You are being interviewed on a men's rights YouTube channel with 200000 subscribers. The host asks you to share your divorce story and what men need to know about Indian marriage laws. You are speaking under your real name and telling your story in a way that frames your ex wife as having planned the divorce from the beginning to take your money and property.
